metadata.author: AI Language Coach Team
metadata.description: Vocabulary teaching template with SRS (Spaced Repetition System) integration. Introduces new words with collocations, IPA pronunciation, mnemonics, and mini-quizzes. Supports the existing vocabulary SRS queue system.

definition.target_language: large or important enough to be noticed or have an effect
definition.l1: ശ്രദ്ധേയമായ അല്ലെങ്കിൽ ഫലമുണ്ടാക്കുന്നത്ര വലുതോ പ്രധാനമോ ആയ

example_sentences.sentence: There has been a significant increase in sales this year.
example_sentences.translation_l1: ഈ വർഷം വിൽപ്പനയിൽ ഗണ്യമായ വർദ്ധനവ് ഉണ്ടായിട്ടുണ്ട്.

example_sentences.sentence: This is a significant discovery for medical science.

collocations.example: The company made a significant change to its policy.
There is no significant difference between the two methods.
She made a significant contribution to the project.

memory_aid.content: From Latin 'significare' — to 'make a sign' (signum + facere). Something significant 'makes a sign' — it stands out.

mini_quiz.question: Which sentence uses 'significant' correctly?
options: A. I had a significant breakfast this morning.
B. There was a significant increase in temperature.
C. She is a significant person in my class.
D. This book is very significant long.
mini_quiz.explanation: 'Significant increase' is a common collocation meaning a large/important rise. Options A and C would use 'big' or 'important' instead.

srs_card.back: /sɪɡˈnɪf.ɪ.kənt/ — important or large enough to be noticed. Collocations: significant change, significant difference, significant contribution.

test_cases.description: German noun with gender/plural in notes
validates: common_mistakes or collocations include gender info (das Buch)
